title: Guard Clause
subtitle: Exit early
goal: Learn to use a Guard Clause to exit unwanted scenarios early.
explanation: A Guard Clause is a check at the beginning of a function.
If a condition is invalid, the function ends immediately.
This helps avoid unnecessary nested if statements.
The code becomes simpler and easier to read.
Guard Clauses are often used to validate access or input data.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: A security guard checks tickets at the entrance. If someone has no ticket, they cannot go any farther.

remember: Use return when requirements are not met.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners write many nested if statements instead of ending the function immediately.

quiz.question: What is a Guard Clause used for?
options: To end an unwanted scenario immediately
